currentlyReading.title: Why Nations Fail?
currentlyReading.author: Daron Acemoglu and James A. Robinson

favorites.title: The Aleph
favorites.author: Jorge Luis Borges
favorites.feedback: too many truths that humanity is not ready to face, and the way it was written made it feel like a dream.

favorites.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
favorites.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
favorites.feedback: for me, it simply was a book that made me feel like i was being punched in the face with every line, and i loved it.

favorites.title: Crime and Punishment
favorites.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
favorites.feedback: mind boggling in such a way that it collapses under the weight of its own complexity

favorites.title: Sputnik Sweetheart
favorites.author: Murakami
favorites.feedback: quiet, confusing kind of loneliness that stays with you even after it ends.

favorites.title: Sophie's World
favorites.author: Jostein Gaarder
favorites.feedback: gave the illustration that i could question thinking itself.

favorites.title: The Stranger
favorites.author: Albert Camus
favorites.feedback: watching life happen without feeling anything, and the absurdity of it all.

favorites.title: सुम्निमा
favorites.author: Bishweshwar Prasad Koirala
favorites.feedback: my favorite nepali read, made my instinct and belief clash, my heart and mind becoming arch nemeses.

favorites.title: अँध्यारोबाट उज्यालोतिर
favorites.author: Ramhari Joshi
favorites.feedback: book that delves into the history of Nepal Congress, and the political turmoil of the time, with a focus on the life and struggles of its leader, Bishweshwar Prasad Koirala.

favorites.title: The Alchemist
favorites.author: Paulo Coelho
favorites.feedback: the purpose to life is not something that can be found, but something that must be created.
